The API-proxy route creates latency and privacy concerns for enterprise customers. Validate willingness-to-pay first — how many users will pay even $1 for 'visibility into what the AI is doing'?

user_feedback.comment: 上下文健康仪表盘本质上是单点功能，防御壁垒不高——大模型厂商随时可以原生集成这个功能。投资价值更多在于它能不能成为更大平台（AI可观测性平台）的入口，而非独立产品长期存活。
user_feedback.comment_en: A context-health dashboard is fundamentally a single feature — weak defensibility. Model vendors can build this natively at any time. The investment value is more about whether this becomes the wedge into a larger platform (AI observability), not whether it survives long-term as a standalone product.

user_feedback.comment: 每天用Claude写长文档的研究员，对这个工具有真实需求。但最关心的不是“技术指标”而是行动建议——系统应该告诉我“现在你需要重申核心要求了”，而不只展示百分比数字。如果只是数字仪表盘，可能看两次就不看了。
user_feedback.comment_en: I'm a researcher writing long documents with Claude daily, and I genuinely need this. But what I actually care about isn't the technical metric — it's the action recommendation. The system should tell me 'now you need to restate the core requirements,' not just show a percentage. If it's just a number dashboard, I'll check it twice and then ignore it.

user_feedback.comment: 浏览器扩展拦截API请求方案可行但维护成本高——每次平台更新接口结构就可能失效。核心算法——什么叫“有效记忆”——也需要认真定义，否则指标会失去可信度。
user_feedback.comment_en: The browser-extension API-interception approach works but has high maintenance cost — every platform UI update can break it. The core algorithm — what counts as 'effective memory' — also needs rigorous definition, or the metric loses credibility.

title: AI编程生产力真实度量工具——打破'感觉更快'的幻觉
title_en: Real AI Coding Productivity Metrics — Break the 'It Feels Faster' Illusion
description: 资深开发者用AI实际慢了19%但自以为快了20%，AI生成的67%的PR被拒绝。整个行业在用主观感受评估AI工具效果。做一个客观的、数据驱动的AI编程生产力度量工具，终结'感觉论'。
description_en: Senior developers using AI are actually 19% slower but believe they're 20% faster. 67% of AI-generated PRs are rejected. The entire industry is evaluating AI tool effectiveness on gut feel. Build an objective, data-driven AI coding productivity measurement tool and end the 'vibes-based' era.
evidence: METR研究：资深开发者用AI实际慢19%
Anthropic报告：67.3% AI PR被拒绝
DORA报告：90%采纳增长=9%bug增长+91%审查时间增长
GitClear：AI代码4倍重复率
evidence_en: METR study: senior developers are 19% slower with AI assistance
Anthropic report: 67.3% of AI-generated PRs are rejected
DORA report: 90% increase in AI adoption correlates with 9% more bugs and 91% more review time
GitClear: AI-generated code has 4x the duplication rate
first_step:
**做什么**：开发一个命令行工具，输入任意Git仓库路径，自动分析过去90天的提交记录，生成一份报告：哪些提交的代码风格高度匹配AI生成模式，以及这些提交的PR通过率和存活天数对比。

**为什么做**：用真实历史数据验证“AI辅助提交的质量是否系统性不同于人工提交”——如果分析结果和METR研究吻合就证明了检测能力有效；如果结论不同本身就是有价值的发现。

**谁来做/怎么做**：一名熟悉Git和Python的后端开发者，1-2周。使用PyDriller解析Git历史，构建启发式规则识别AI生成代码特征，结合GitHub/GitLab API拉取PR状态数据，输出Markdown格式报告。以5-10个开源项目的历史数据做初步验证。
first_step_en:
**What**: Build a CLI that takes any Git repo path, auto-analyzes the last 90 days of commits, and produces a report: which commits' code style closely matches AI-generation patterns, plus PR acceptance rate and survival-in-days comparison between those and manual commits.

**Why**: Use real historical data to validate whether 'AI-assisted commits systematically differ in quality from manual ones.' If findings match METR's study, detection is working. If they differ, that itself is a valuable finding.

**Who/How**: One backend developer experienced with Git and Python, 1-2 weeks. Use PyDriller to parse Git history, build heuristics to identify AI-generated code signatures, pull PR state data via GitHub/GitLab APIs, output a Markdown report. Run initial validation against 5-10 open-source projects.
cc_anchor: 10_Token经济学.md — CC自身的token成本追踪方法 + 可观测性章节的数据驱动决策
cc_anchor_en: 10_Token_Economics.md — CC's own token cost tracking methodology + data-driven decisions from the observability chapter
plain_explanation: 有个研究发现让很多人惊讶：一批经验丰富的程序员用了AI辅助工具后，实际完成任务的速度比不用AI慢了近两成——但他们自己觉得快了两成。就像你买了号称“省油”的新车，感觉每次加油都更放心了，但因为开车变得更随意了总油耗反而涨了。现在想做的是给每个用AI工具的程序员配一个“真实行车记录仪”：客观记录每天写了多少代码、多少通过了审查、多少被退回重写、真正节省了多少时间。用真实数据代替感觉。
plain_explanation_en: A surprising research finding: a group of experienced programmers using AI assistance were actually nearly 20% slower at completing tasks — but they themselves believed they were 20% faster. Like buying a new car advertised as 'fuel-efficient' — you feel better at every fill-up, but because you drive more casually, your total fuel consumption actually went up. The idea here is to give every developer using AI tools a 'real drive recorder' — objectively logging how much code was written, how much passed review, how much got sent back for rewrites, and how much time was actually saved. Replace gut feel with real data.
why_it_matters:
**市场需求**：企业软件工程团队正面临AI工具采购决策压力——CTO需要向董事会证明AI工具订阅预算值得，但目前市场上根本没有客观度量工具，只有厂商自己的宣传数据。

**竞争空白**：现有工程效能工具（DORA指标、LinearB、Jellyfish）不区分“AI辅助”和“人工完成”的提交。这个细分维度目前几乎空白。

**时机窗口**：2025-2026年正是企业AI工具采购从“探索期”进入“续费决策期”的节点——第一批客户用了一两年Copilot/Cursor，现在需要决定是否续费。
why_it_matters_en:
**Market Need**: Enterprise engineering teams are under AI-tool procurement pressure — CTOs need to show their board that the AI tool subscription was worth it, and the market currently offers no objective measurement, only vendor marketing data.

**Competitive Gap**: Existing engineering-efficiency tools (DORA metrics, LinearB, Jellyfish) don't distinguish 'AI-assisted' from 'manually written' commits. This dimension is essentially empty.

**Timing Window**: 2025-2026 is exactly the transition point where enterprise AI tool purchases move from 'exploration' to 'renewal decision' — the first wave of customers has used Copilot/Cursor for a year or two and now has to decide whether to renew.

user_feedback.comment: 方向对但切入点要谨慎。面向个人开发者很难变现；面向企业必须解决数据权限问题——在员工代码提交上打标签会遇到工会和隐私阻力。建议从自愿参与的个人社区开始积累数据，形成行业基准报告，再以“基准对标服务”向企业收费。
user_feedback.comment_en: Right direction, but be careful about the wedge. Individual developers are hard to monetize; enterprise requires solving the data-rights problem — labeling employee commits runs into union and privacy resistance. Start by aggregating data from a voluntary individual community, publish it as an industry benchmark report, then sell 'benchmark comparison as a service' to enterprises.

user_feedback.comment: METR研究和Anthropic数据是强信号，说明市场存在真实的“信息不对称”商业价值。但要警惕：一旦GitHub Copilot、Cursor等厂商开始提供官方效能报告，第三方工具差异化就很难维持。护城河必须建在“独立性”和“跨工具横向比较”上。
user_feedback.comment_en: The METR study and Anthropic data are strong signals — there's real 'information asymmetry' value in this market. But beware: once GitHub Copilot, Cursor, and others start publishing official effectiveness reports, third-party differentiation becomes hard to sustain. The moat has to be built on independence and cross-tool horizontal comparison.

user_feedback.comment: 有点担心这个工具会变成管理层监控手段。如果公司强制接入会本能抵触。但如果自己选择用、数据只有自己看，反而很想知道自己用Cursor到底快了多少。工具的隐私设计和所有权归属比功能本身更重要。
user_feedback.comment_en: I worry this tool becomes a management surveillance weapon. If my company mandates it, I'd instinctively resist. But if I opt in myself and only I see the data, I'd genuinely like to know how much faster I actually am with Cursor. The privacy design and data ownership matter more than the feature set itself.

user_feedback.comment: 最大挑战是“AI辅助”的定义——一行代码如果是AI生成但被人大幅修改，算AI的还是人的？Git历史只能区分谁提交了，无法区分谁写了。需要IDE插件层面埋点才能准确追踪，数据采集链路比看起来复杂得多。
user_feedback.comment_en: The hardest question is defining 'AI-assisted' — if a line was generated by AI and then heavily modified by a human, whose is it? Git history only tells you who committed, not who wrote. Accurate tracking requires instrumentation at the IDE plugin level, and the data-collection pipeline is much more complex than it looks.

title: 人机协作UX范式——从'审批疲劳'到'智能信任阶梯'
title_en: Human-AI Collaboration UX: From Approval Fatigue to a Smart Trust Ladder
description: 93%的权限提示被无脑批准，76%的开发者不信任AI重构但76%也拒绝让AI碰生产环境。问题不是'要不要给AI权限'，而是'怎么让信任随经验增长'。做一个渐进式信任系统，新操作严格审批，成功10次后自动放宽。
description_en: 93% of permission prompts are blindly approved. 76% of developers don't trust AI refactoring, and 76% also refuse to let AI touch production. The real question isn't 'should AI have permission' — it's 'how does trust grow with experience.' Build a progressive trust system: strict approval for new operations, automatic relaxation after 10 successes.
evidence: SPK-101 93%权限被无脑批准说明二元审批失败
SPK-143 76%拒绝AI碰生产环境说明信任有边界
CC的Iron Gate连续拒绝降级机制是反向案例
SPK-206的5字母微确认协议是轻量级人在环的最佳实践
evidence_en: SPK-101 — 93% of permissions are blindly approved, proving binary approval has failed
SPK-143 — 76% refuse to let AI touch production, proving trust has boundaries
CC's Iron Gate consecutive-denial demotion is a negative-example case
SPK-206's 5-letter micro-confirmation protocol is the best practice for lightweight human-in-the-loop
first_step:
**做什么**：为Claude Code的Hook系统开发一个“信任记录器”插件——在每次工具调用前后记录操作类型、用户是否批准、操作是否成功，经过30天积累后生成一份“哪些操作类型值得自动授权”的推荐报告。

**为什么做**：先收集真实的人类-AI协作数据，验证“重复操作确实存在规律性”这个核心前提——如果数据显示某类操作被100%批准从未出错，就为动态信任算法提供了实证基础。

**谁来做/怎么做**：一名熟悉Claude Code Hook机制的开发者，1周完成数据收集版本。利用PreToolUse和PostToolUse Hook事件，将操作记录写入本地SQLite数据库，用Python脚本定期生成操作模式分析报告，暂不实现自动授权只做数据积累和可视化。
first_step_en:
**What**: Build a 'trust logger' plugin for Claude Code's Hook system — before and after every tool call, record operation type, whether the user approved, and whether it succeeded. After 30 days of data, generate a report recommending which operation types deserve auto-authorization.

**Why**: Collect real human-AI collaboration data first to validate the core premise that 'repeat operations exhibit regular patterns.' If data shows a class of operation is 100% approved with zero errors, we have empirical grounding for a dynamic-trust algorithm.

**Who/How**: One developer familiar with Claude Code's Hook mechanism, 1 week for the data-collection version. Use PreToolUse and PostToolUse hook events, write operation records to a local SQLite database, use a Python script to periodically generate operation-pattern reports. No auto-authorization yet — just data accumulation and visualization.
cc_anchor: 04_多层防线.md — denialTracking.ts连续拒绝降级 + channelPermissions.ts 5字母微确认 + autoAllowBashIfSandboxed技术信任替代
cc_anchor_en: 04_Multi-Layer_Defense.md — denialTracking.ts consecutive-denial demotion + channelPermissions.ts 5-letter micro-confirmation + autoAllowBashIfSandboxed technical-trust substitute
plain_explanation: 你刚雇了一个新助手，第一天当然要手把手盯着每一步——帮你发邮件前要先看一遍，帮你订会议室前要先确认。但如果每次发邮件他都停下来问你，三个月后你会抓狂。聪明的管理方式是：做了十次都对的事就不再过问；第一次做新事情或高风险操作才重新严格审查。现在的AI工具不是这样——不管合作多久每次动文件都弹确认框，结果大家养成了“反正就点确认”的坏习惯。这个蓝图要做一套“你可以逐渐信任我”的系统，让AI权限随着良好记录自动进化。
plain_explanation_en: When you hire a new assistant, of course you watch every step on day one — review each email before it goes, confirm each meeting room before it's booked. But if they stop and ask you every time after three months, you'll lose your mind. The smart management approach is: once something's been done right ten times, stop asking; only re-scrutinize for new tasks or high-risk operations. Today's AI tools do the opposite — every file touch triggers a confirmation dialog regardless of how long you've been working together. The result: everyone has trained themselves to just click 'confirm' without thinking. This blueprint builds a 'you can trust me gradually' system, where AI permissions auto-evolve based on a track record.
why_it_matters:
**市场需求**：93%的AI权限提示被无脑批准说明当前二元式权限设计已经失效——制造了虚假安全感同时制造了真实工作流摩擦。这是系统性UX失败。

**竞争空白**：当前所有主流AI编程工具（Cursor、Copilot、Claude Code）的权限系统都是简单开关或一次性授权，没有任何工具实现基于使用历史的动态信任模型。

**时机窗口**：随着AI Agent开始执行高风险操作（部署代码、修改数据库），“如何合理授权”成为企业采购AI工具的核心顾虑。谁先提供既安全又不繁琐的信任框架，就占领企业用户心智。
why_it_matters_en:
**Market Need**: 93% blind approval of AI permission prompts proves the current binary permission model has failed — it creates false security while generating real workflow friction. This is systematic UX failure.

**Competitive Gap**: Every mainstream AI coding tool today (Cursor, Copilot, Claude Code) uses simple toggles or one-time authorization. Not one implements a dynamic trust model based on usage history.

**Timing Window**: As AI agents start performing high-stakes operations (code deployment, database modification), 'how do we authorize this reasonably' becomes the core concern in enterprise AI tool procurement. Whoever ships a trust framework that's both safe and non-burdensome captures enterprise mindshare.

user_feedback.comment: 商业路径相对清晰——作为SDK卖给企业AI工具开发商或中间件插入现有平台。但核心挑战是标准化：不同企业对“安全操作”定义差异极大，很难做通用信任评分模型。早期可能需要深度绑定一两个垂直行业做定制再泛化。
user_feedback.comment_en: The commercial path is reasonably clear — sell as an SDK to enterprise AI tool vendors, or insert as middleware into existing platforms. The core challenge is standardization: different enterprises define 'safe operation' very differently, and a universal trust-scoring model is hard to build. Early on, you may need to deeply partner with one or two vertical industries for custom implementations, then generalize.

user_feedback.comment: 最有可能成为“隐形基础层”的机会之一。一旦信任框架成为行业默认标准，切换成本极高护城河很深。但要警惕研究论文化风险——容易停在学术讨论层面。必须有一个“强制落地”的场景。
user_feedback.comment_en: One of the most likely 'invisible foundation layer' opportunities. Once a trust framework becomes industry default, switching costs are enormous and the moat is deep. But watch the 'research-paper trap' — ideas like this easily stall in academic-discussion mode. You need a forcing-function scenario that compels real-world deployment.

user_feedback.comment: 最担心的是：如果系统自动“升级”AI权限出了问题谁负责？作为工程团队负责人，需要能随时说清楚“为什么AI有权限动这个文件”。信任阶梯的每一步都需要清晰审计日志，不然合规部门不会放行。
user_feedback.comment_en: My biggest concern: if the system auto-escalates AI permissions and something goes wrong, who's responsible? As an engineering team lead, I need to be able to explain on demand 'why does the AI have permission to touch this file.' Every rung of the trust ladder needs a clear audit log, or compliance will never sign off.

user_feedback.comment: CC的denialTracking.ts实现其实很简单——只是计数连续拒绝次数。真正的信任阶梯需要更复杂的状态机：操作类型分级、成功率加权、时间衰减、异常操作触发降级。如何在不同企业环境中校准参数是真正的工程挑战。
user_feedback.comment_en: CC's denialTracking.ts implementation is actually very simple — it just counts consecutive denials. A real trust ladder needs a much more complex state machine: operation-type tiers, success-rate weighting, time decay, anomalous-operation demotion triggers. Calibrating parameters across different enterprise environments is the real engineering challenge.

title: AI源码解剖即服务——企业级AI工具独立评测
title_en: AI Source Autopsy as a Service: Independent Enterprise AI Tool Evaluation
description: 我们证明了从1884个文件到30万字深度分析的方法是可复现的。把这套方法论变成一个服务：企业CTO想评估一个AI工具，我们提供源码行号级精度的独立第三方深度报告。Gartner靠猜，我们靠源码。
description_en: We've proven that a methodology scaling from 1,884 files to 300,000 words of deep analysis is reproducible. Turn that methodology into a service: when enterprise CTOs want to evaluate an AI tool, we deliver an independent third-party deep report with line-number-level source precision. Gartner guesses. We read source.
evidence: 本项目自身就是存在证明：30万字+120个洞察
SPK-012 内部版vs公开版差异分析有巨大信息价值
SPK-128 反蒸馏标志等不透明行为需要独立审计
企业CTO缺乏评估AI工具技术成熟度的独立信源
evidence_en: This project itself is the existence proof: 300,000 words + 120 insights
SPK-012 — the internal-versus-public version diff analysis has enormous informational value
SPK-128 — opaque behaviors like anti-distillation markers require independent auditing
Enterprise CTOs lack independent sources for evaluating AI tool technical maturity
first_step:
**做什么**：以本Claude Code分析项目为素材，整理一份公开的“AI工具源码评测方法论白皮书”，包含评测维度框架、分析流程SOP、报告模板，并发布为免费内容，同时附上付费报告的服务说明。

**为什么做**：免费白皮书是最高效的获客漏斗——有需求的CTO会主动搜到它，看完后对付费报告产生兴趣；同时写作过程本身会迫使方法论结构化。

**谁来做/怎么做**：本项目核心作者直接操刀，2周。输出：1份2万字方法论白皮书（含评测维度定义、Claude Code实战案例片段、报告结构模板）+ 1页服务介绍落地页。通过Hacker News、独立开发者社群发布。
first_step_en:
**What**: Using this Claude Code analysis project as the source material, package a public 'Methodology for Evaluating AI Tool Source Code' white paper covering the evaluation-dimension framework, analysis-flow SOP, and report templates. Publish for free, with a service description attached for paid reports.

**Why**: A free white paper is the highest-efficiency acquisition funnel — CTOs with real need will find it organically, read it, and warm up to the paid report. The writing process itself forces the methodology into structured form.

**Who/How**: Core authors of this project, 2 weeks. Deliverables: a 20,000-word methodology white paper (evaluation-dimension definitions, Claude Code case-study excerpts, report structure templates) plus a one-page service landing page. Distribute via Hacker News and independent developer communities.
cc_anchor: meta层面 — 本项目的方法论本身：逐文件逐函数级分析 + 源码行号精度(如memdir.ts第57-103行) + 多Agent协作生产
cc_anchor_en: Meta-level — the methodology of this project itself: file-by-file, function-by-function analysis + source-line precision (e.g., memdir.ts lines 57-103) + multi-agent collaborative production
plain_explanation: 想象你要买一台昂贵的医疗设备，厂商说它有多好多好，但你作为医院院长不可能完全相信厂商的自我评价。你需要一份独立的、权威的第三方检测报告，说清楚这台机器的实际性能边界、已知缺陷、适用和不适用场景。现在AI工具市场缺的正是这种独立评测——每个厂商都说自己最好，没有人用“打开机器盖子看里面怎么转”的方式做客观报告。我们做Claude Code源码分析等于证明了“拆机评测法”可行：从原始代码出发写出行号级精准的深度分析。现在要把这套方法论包装成服务。
plain_explanation_en: Imagine buying an expensive piece of medical equipment. The manufacturer tells you how great it is, but as the hospital director you can't fully trust a self-assessment. You need an independent, authoritative third-party report spelling out the machine's real performance boundaries, known defects, and suitable versus unsuitable scenarios. What the AI tool market lacks today is exactly this kind of independent evaluation — every vendor claims to be the best, and no one does the 'open the machine and see how it actually works' style of objective reporting. Our Claude Code source analysis proved the 'teardown evaluation method' is viable: starting from raw code, we produced line-number-precise deep analysis. Now we package that methodology as a service.
why_it_matters:
**市场需求**：企业AI工具采购正从“试点”进入“规模化部署”，决策金额从万元跳升到百万千万级。在这个量级CTO需要独立信源，而目前不存在这样的服务。

**竞争空白**：独立安全审计（SOC2、渗透测试）是成熟市场，但“AI工具技术架构深度评测”几乎空白。没有公认机构、没有标准方法论——先入者可以定义标准。

**时机窗口**：AI工具的“源码可获得性”正在成为企业采购隐性要求——越来越多安全部门要求审查AI工具实现细节。同时AI工具市场正处于快速整合期（2025-2026年），企业CTO面临“选Copilot还是Cursor还是Claude Code”的重大决策，迫切需要独立第三方深度对比报告。本项目恰好在这个时机点证明了方法论的可行性。
why_it_matters_en:
**Market Need**: Enterprise AI tool procurement is moving from 'pilots' to 'scaled deployment,' and decision amounts are jumping from thousands to millions of dollars. At this scale CTOs need independent sources — and no such service exists today.

**Competitive Gap**: Independent security auditing (SOC 2, penetration testing) is a mature market, but 'AI tool technical architecture deep evaluation' is nearly empty. No recognized authority, no standard methodology — first movers get to define the standard.

**Timing Window**: AI tool 'source code availability' is becoming an implicit enterprise procurement requirement — more security departments are demanding to review implementation details. Meanwhile, the AI tool market is in a rapid consolidation phase (2025-2026), with enterprise CTOs facing 'Copilot or Cursor or Claude Code' decisions and urgently needing independent third-party deep comparison reports. This project has proven methodological feasibility at exactly this inflection point.

user_feedback.comment: 少数几个“现金流可以很快出现”的方向——服务型业务不需要等产品打磨期。但人力密集是硬伤：每份深度报告可能需要数周投入，难以规模化。要做成公司而非个人顾问，必须解决方法论的可复制性问题。
user_feedback.comment_en: One of the few directions where cash flow can appear quickly — services don't require a product-polishing period. But the labor intensity is brutal: each deep report takes weeks to produce and doesn't scale. To build a company rather than a solo consultancy, you have to solve the methodology-reproducibility problem.

user_feedback.comment: 高利润低规模业务——每个客户付费高但数量天花板明显。如果定位成精品顾问每年10-20个大客户完全可以支撑小团队，但不太适合VC逻辑。更有价值的形态是把方法论做成自动化平台，人工只负责最终审查。
user_feedback.comment_en: High-margin, low-volume business — each customer pays a lot but the count ceiling is low. Positioned as a boutique consultancy with 10-20 large clients per year, it can easily support a small team, but it doesn't fit VC math. The more investable form is productizing the methodology as an automated platform where humans only do final review.

user_feedback.comment: 作为金融公司CTO确实需要这种报告，但有核心顾虑：能评测的前提是源码可获得，但大多数AI工具（包括OpenAI、Anthropic的API）是完全黑盒的。如果报告只能覆盖开源或泄露源码的工具，实际能帮到决策的场景就有限了。
user_feedback.comment_en: As a CTO at a financial firm, I genuinely need this kind of report, but I have a core concern: the evaluation presupposes source availability, yet most AI tools (including OpenAI's and Anthropic's APIs) are complete black boxes. If the report only covers open-source or leaked-source tools, the decision-relevant coverage is narrow.

user_feedback.comment: 本项目方法论是真实可用的，但需要标准化才能成为服务产品。目前分析流程依赖特定人的判断——哪些代码值得深入、哪些设计有问题——这些是经验依赖的。要做成SOP需要把分析框架拆解成可量化检查清单。
user_feedback.comment_en: The project's methodology genuinely works, but it needs to be standardized to become a productized service. The current analysis flow relies on specific individuals' judgment — which code is worth deep-diving, which designs are suspect — all experience-dependent. Turning this into an SOP means decomposing the analysis framework into quantifiable checklists.

title: Dream记忆整合通用引擎——知识工作者的'夜间自动整理'
title_en: Dream Consolidation Engine: Overnight Auto-Organization for Knowledge Workers
description: CC的Dream机制在空闲时自动整理跨会话的记忆碎片。人类知识工作者同样需要但永远不做这件事。做一个通用引擎，接入Obsidian/Notion/日历/邮件，每天夜间自动整合产出'今日洞察'和'跨项目关联'。
description_en: CC's Dream mechanism automatically consolidates cross-session memory fragments during idle time. Human knowledge workers need the same thing but never actually do it. Build a general-purpose engine that plugs into Obsidian, Notion, calendar, and email, consolidating every night to produce 'today's insights' and 'cross-project connections.'
evidence: CC的autoDream.ts验证了时间门控+信息密度门控+后台执行的模式
SPK-201教育场景验证了Dream机制跨领域迁移的可行性
40%的Agent项目失败根因是记忆管理(SPK-149)
知识工作者有'整理笔记'的刚需但执行率极低
evidence_en: CC's autoDream.ts validated the pattern of time gating + information-density gating + background execution
SPK-201 — education scenario validates the cross-domain transfer of the Dream mechanism
40% of agent project failures root-cause to memory management (SPK-149)
Knowledge workers have strong demand for 'organizing notes' but extremely low execution rate
first_step:
**做什么**：开发一个Obsidian插件，每天早上启动Obsidian时（而非夜间后台运行），自动扫描过去24小时新建或修改的笔记，调用本地Ollama或Claude API生成一段200字以内的“昨日要点与关联提示”，显示在侧边栏。

**为什么做**：用“早晨启动时触发”替代“夜间后台常驻”，绕开进程守护和设备休眠的技术难点，快速验证最核心的价值假设——AI自动生成的跨笔记关联是否对用户有实际价值。

**谁来做/怎么做**：一名熟悉TypeScript和Obsidian插件开发的开发者，2周完成可用版本。Obsidian插件API读取最近修改文件列表，构建整合提示词，调用LLM API生成摘要，以自己的Obsidian库作为第一个测试环境。
first_step_en:
**What**: Build an Obsidian plugin that, on morning Obsidian startup (not as a nighttime background process), auto-scans notes created or modified in the last 24 hours, calls local Ollama or the Claude API to generate a ≤200-word 'yesterday's key points and connection prompts,' and displays it in the sidebar.

**Why**: Using 'trigger at morning startup' instead of 'resident nighttime background process' sidesteps the technical pain of process persistence and device sleep, letting us quickly validate the core value hypothesis — whether AI-generated cross-note connections actually matter to users.

**Who/How**: One developer familiar with TypeScript and Obsidian plugin development, 2 weeks to a usable version. Use the Obsidian plugin API to read the recently-modified-file list, construct the consolidation prompt, call the LLM API to generate summaries. Dogfood on your own Obsidian vault as the first test environment.
cc_anchor: autoDream.ts — 三道门槛触发(≥24h + ≥5 sessions + 无并发锁) + runForkedAgent()隔离执行 + 四阶段(orient→gather→consolidate→prune)
cc_anchor_en: autoDream.ts — three-threshold trigger (≥24h + ≥5 sessions + no concurrency lock) + runForkedAgent() isolated execution + four phases (orient → gather → consolidate → prune)
plain_explanation: 你每天工作结束后，桌上堆着一堆便利贴、一个打了一半的文档、三个没发出去的草稿邮件、还有十几个没读完的文章链接。这些东西单独看都不完整，但如果有人帮你在晚上整理一遍，告诉你“今天这三件事其实是同一个问题的不同侧面”“你昨天写的思路和上周看的文章可以结合起来”——第二天早上你看到的不是乱摊子而是一份精炼的“今日洞察”。Claude Code有一个叫Dream的机制在用户不用电脑时自动做这个整理。现在想做的就是把这个“夜间大脑整理员”做成通用工具，接入你平时用的笔记软件、日历、邮件。
plain_explanation_en: At the end of a workday, your desk has a pile of sticky notes, a half-finished document, three unsent draft emails, and a dozen unread article links. None of them feel complete on their own, but if someone organized them overnight and told you 'these three things are actually different facets of the same problem' or 'the idea you wrote yesterday combines with the article you read last week' — the next morning you'd see a crisp summary instead of a mess. Claude Code has a mechanism called Dream that does this automatically when the user isn't at the computer. The goal here is to turn that 'overnight brain organizer' into a general-purpose tool connected to your note-taking app, calendar, and email.
why_it_matters:
**市场需求**：知识工作者面临“信息摄入过载、整合能力不足”的普遍困境。现有工具（Notion、Obsidian、Roam）解决“存储和检索”问题，但没有工具解决“自动整合与关联”问题。

**竞争空白**：Mem.ai聚焦搜索，Reflect聚焦双链笔记，Rewind专注记录回放——没有产品以“跨应用夜间整合”为核心价值主张。

**时机窗口**：Obsidian插件生态和Notion API的成熟度在2024-2025年达到支撑“夜间Agent”类应用的临界点；同时本地大模型（Ollama）解决了私密笔记数据上传云端的隐私顾虑。
why_it_matters_en:
**Market Need**: Knowledge workers universally face 'information overload plus consolidation deficit.' Existing tools (Notion, Obsidian, Roam) solve storage and retrieval, but no tool solves auto-consolidation and connection-finding.

**Competitive Gap**: Mem.ai focuses on search, Reflect on bidirectional-link notes, Rewind on recording replay. No product's core value proposition is 'cross-application overnight consolidation.'

**Timing Window**: Obsidian's plugin ecosystem and Notion's API matured enough in 2024-2025 to support nighttime-agent applications. Simultaneously, local LLMs (Ollama) resolved the privacy concern around uploading private note data to the cloud.

user_feedback.comment: 变现路径不清晰是最大风险。知识管理工具历史上很难收费——用户觉得这是“应该免费的”基础功能。Readwise是少数成功案例但它靠精准的卡片复习功能。夜间整合的价值主张——“帮你发现关联”——需要更大力气让用户相信值得付费。
user_feedback.comment_en: Unclear monetization is the biggest risk. Knowledge-management tools have historically been hard to charge for — users feel this should be a free baseline feature. Readwise is one of the rare successes, but it earned that by nailing precise flashcard review. The 'help you find connections' value proposition of overnight consolidation will take significant effort to convince users is worth paying for.

user_feedback.comment: 知识管理工具赛道在2022-2023年被过度投资了，很多产品死在“用户粘性不足”上。用户刚开始觉得神奇，但一两个月后整合出的“洞察”质量不稳定就放弃了。更适合作为大产品的功能模块而非早期创业切入点。
user_feedback.comment_en: The knowledge-management space was over-funded in 2022-2023, and many products died on 'insufficient user retention.' Users find it magical at first, but within a month or two, when the 'insights' quality becomes uneven, they quit. This is better positioned as a feature module of a larger product than as an early-stage startup wedge.

user_feedback.comment: 用Obsidian两年了，每次试新整合工具的体验都是前两周觉得太酷了，第三周发现“整合”出的关联大多数是我早就知道的废话，第四周关掉。最大挑战是“洞察质量”——如果AI只是把相似主题笔记凑在一起而不是发现反直觉的关联，宁愿自己整理。
user_feedback.comment_en: I've used Obsidian for two years, and every time I try a new consolidation tool the pattern is the same: weeks 1-2 feel magical, week 3 I realize most of the 'connections' are obvious stuff I already knew, week 4 I turn it off. The core challenge is insight quality — if AI just groups similar-topic notes together rather than surfacing counterintuitive connections, I'd rather organize them myself.

user_feedback.comment: autoDream.ts是有启发性的参考，但它的触发条件在个人设备上实现比服务器端复杂——需要处理设备睡眠、网络中断、进程守护等问题。Obsidian插件API限制也比较多，后台常驻进程在移动端几乎不可用。纯桌面端场景可行但覆盖范围窄。
user_feedback.comment_en: autoDream.ts is a useful reference, but implementing its trigger conditions on personal devices is harder than on the server side — you have to handle device sleep, network interruption, process persistence, etc. Obsidian's plugin API is also relatively limited, and background-resident processes are essentially unavailable on mobile. Desktop-only is feasible but the coverage is narrow.

title: 企业AI治理分层策略平台——AI Policy Hub
title_en: AI Policy Hub: Enterprise AI Governance with Layered Policy Management
description: 企业部署AI最头疼的事：IT想管住底线但不想妨碍开发者灵活性。CC的四层CLAUDE.md加载机制精确解决了这个矛盾。把它产品化成SaaS——IT管理员在控制台写'不准在代码中硬编码密码'，这条策略自动注入所有开发者的AI工具。
description_en: The biggest headache in enterprise AI deployment: IT wants to lock down the bottom line without hampering developer flexibility. CC's four-layer CLAUDE.md loading mechanism solves exactly this tension. Productize it as SaaS — an IT admin writes 'no hard-coded passwords in code' in the console, and that policy auto-injects into every developer's AI tooling.
evidence: CC的Managed→User→Project→Local四层机制已在生产环境验证
SPK-234的追加vs替换语义解决了配置合并难题
SPK-225的声明式优先级解决了配置冲突调试问题
企业AI治理目前靠政策文档+人工审查效率极低
evidence_en: CC's Managed → User → Project → Local four-layer mechanism has been validated in production
SPK-234's append-versus-replace semantics solves the config-merge problem
SPK-225's declarative priority solves the config-conflict debugging problem
Enterprise AI governance today relies on policy documents plus manual review — extremely inefficient
first_step:
**做什么**：构建一个命令行工具ai-policy-lint，输入三层配置文件（企业策略YAML+项目级CLAUDE.md+个人配置），合并后输出“最终生效策略”全览+冲突报告+覆盖链追踪，让管理员一眼看清“为什么这条规则没生效”。

**为什么做**：企业AI治理的第一痛点不是“制定策略”而是“确认策略生效了”。一个能可视化策略合并结果的工具，立刻能帮安全团队回答审计时最常被问的问题：“你怎么确保每个开发者的AI工具都遵守了公司策略？”

**谁来做/怎么做**：一名Node.js工程师，2周完成CLI v0.1。参考CLAUDE.md四层加载优先级逻辑，实现配置合并引擎+冲突检测+覆盖链追踪，输出彩色终端报告。以3个模拟企业场景做demo，在企业架构师社群发布收集反馈。
first_step_en:
**What**: Build a CLI called `ai-policy-lint` that takes three layers of config (enterprise policy YAML + project-level CLAUDE.md + personal config), merges them, and outputs a 'final effective policy' overview, conflict report, and override-chain trace — so admins can see at a glance 'why did this rule not take effect.'

**Why**: The top pain point in enterprise AI governance isn't 'defining policy' — it's 'confirming policy actually took effect.' A tool that visualizes merged policy results instantly lets security teams answer the most common audit question: 'How do you ensure every developer's AI tool complies with company policy?'

**Who/How**: One Node.js engineer, 2 weeks to CLI v0.1. Model on CLAUDE.md's four-layer loading priority logic; implement the config-merge engine, conflict detection, and override-chain tracing; produce colored terminal reports. Demo with 3 simulated enterprise scenarios, release in enterprise-architect communities for feedback.

cc_anchor_en: 06_CLAUDE_md_Loading_System_Deep_Dive.md — four-layer loading priority + isClaudeMdExcluded() non-excludable mechanism + 11_Configuration_Governance.md nine-layer settings system
plain_explanation: 想象一个连锁学校系统：教育局规定“所有学校不能体罚学生”——这是铁律，任何校长都不能推翻。但每个区可以根据本地情况加自己的规则（“本区学校每天至少一小时户外活动”），每个学校还可以制定自己的特色课程安排。层层叠加，但底线不可动摇。企业管理AI工具的需求一模一样：IT部门要守住安全底线（“不准在代码里写密码”），但又不想限制每个开发者按自己的习惯配置AI助手。Claude Code有一套精巧的四层规则系统完美解决了这个矛盾。BLP-018要做的就是把这套规则系统做成一个管理平台，让企业IT像教育局管理学校一样——守住底线的同时给每一层足够的自主空间。
plain_explanation_en: Imagine a district school system: the board of education rules that 'no school may use corporal punishment' — that's an iron rule, no principal can override it. But each district can add its own rules based on local conditions ('schools in this district need at least one hour outdoors daily'), and each school can design its own specialty curriculum. Layers stack, but the bottom line doesn't move. Enterprise AI tool management has the identical shape: IT needs to hold the security floor ('no passwords in code') without constraining how each developer configures their AI assistant. Claude Code has an elegant four-layer rule system that solves this tension. BLP-018 turns that system into a management platform — letting enterprise IT manage like a school board: hold the floor while giving every layer room to breathe.
why_it_matters:
**市场需求**：企业AI采用率飙升但治理工具严重滞后。IT安全团队面临的典型困境是：知道需要规范AI工具使用，但找不到技术手段强制执行。目前唯一的办法是发政策邮件+定期人工抽查——效率极低且漏洞百出。

**竞争空白**：MDM（移动设备管理）是成熟市场，但“AI工具策略管理”这个类目几乎不存在。没有产品能做到“IT写一条规则，自动注入所有员工的AI工具”。Claude Code的四层加载机制证明了技术可行性，但还没有人把它产品化。

**时机窗口**：2025-2026年企业AI部署从试点进入全面推广，CTO/CISO同时面临“加速采用”和“保障合规”的双重压力。能同时满足两个需求的治理工具将成为必选项。
why_it_matters_en:
**Market Need**: Enterprise AI adoption is surging, but governance tooling is badly lagging. The typical IT security dilemma: they know they need to standardize AI tool usage but can't find a technical way to enforce it. The only current approach is policy emails plus periodic manual spot-checks — extremely inefficient and full of holes.

**Competitive Gap**: MDM (mobile device management) is a mature market, but 'AI tool policy management' barely exists as a category. No product today can deliver 'IT writes one rule, it auto-injects into every employee's AI tools.' Claude Code's four-layer loading proves technical feasibility, but no one has productized it.

**Timing Window**: In 2025-2026, enterprise AI deployment moves from pilot to full rollout, and CTOs/CISOs face simultaneous pressure to 'accelerate adoption' and 'ensure compliance.' Governance tools that satisfy both needs become must-haves.

user_feedback.comment: ToB安全合规赛道客单价高、粘性强，但销售周期长，从接触到签约可能6-12个月。CLI工具是好的敲门砖但转化路径需要设计：免费CLI积累用户→识别企业用户→提供Web控制台+团队管理的付费版。早期建议瞄准100-500人的科技公司，它们有合规压力但还没有专职安全团队。
user_feedback.comment_en: B2B security-compliance has high ACV and strong retention, but long sales cycles — 6 to 12 months from first contact to signed contract. The CLI tool is a good icebreaker, but the conversion path needs design: free CLI accumulates users → identify enterprise users → upsell to a paid Web console with team management. Early-stage, target 100-500 person tech companies — they have compliance pressure but no dedicated security team yet.

user_feedback.comment: AI治理是2025-2026年确定性最高的企业软件细分之一，Gartner预测AI治理工具市场年增长超60%。核心评估点是：团队能否快速适配主流AI工具（不只Claude Code还要支持Cursor、Copilot等），以及能否在Anthropic/OpenAI自己推出企业策略管理功能之前建立足够大的客户基础。
user_feedback.comment_en: AI governance is one of the most certain enterprise software subcategories for 2025-2026. Gartner forecasts the AI governance tools market growing 60%+ annually. The core evaluation question: can the team move fast enough to support mainstream AI tools (not just Claude Code but Cursor, Copilot, etc.), and can they build a large enough customer base before Anthropic or OpenAI ship their own enterprise policy management.

user_feedback.comment: 作为200人团队的IT负责人，我最想要的是：在一个地方写下“禁止在AI对话中粘贴客户PII数据”，然后这条规则能自动生效到所有人的Claude Code和Cursor上。如果只支持Claude Code一个工具，对我来说用处有限——我们团队用了4种不同的AI工具，需要统一管理。
user_feedback.comment_en: As IT lead for a 200-person team, what I want most: I write 'never paste customer PII into AI chat' in one place, and it auto-takes-effect on everyone's Claude Code *and* Cursor. If it only supports Claude Code, the value to me is limited — our team uses 4 different AI tools and I need to manage them together.

user_feedback.comment: CC的四层加载逻辑是极好的设计参考，但产品化时最大的工程挑战是“注入”环节——如何把企业策略可靠地注入到每个员工本地安装的AI工具中？CLAUDE.md是本地文件，企业没有远程推送能力。可能需要一个轻量级Agent常驻在员工机器上同步策略，这涉及到企业软件分发和权限管理的复杂问题。
user_feedback.comment_en: CC's four-layer loading logic is a great design reference, but the hardest engineering problem in productization is the injection step — how do you reliably inject enterprise policy into every employee's locally-installed AI tools? CLAUDE.md is a local file; enterprises don't have remote-push capability. You may need a lightweight agent resident on employee machines to sync policy, which opens the full can of worms around enterprise software distribution and permission management.

title: AI代码质量防线——跨文件一致性守卫Agent
title_en: AI Code Quality Guard: Cross-File Consistency Agent
description: AI改了1个文件但忘了另外19个相关文件。AI代码的重复率是人类的4倍。65%的开发者说AI重构会遗漏上下文。做一个专门的'一致性守卫Agent'——每次AI修改后自动扫描全局不变量、类型一致性、API契约，在PR提交前拦截问题。
description_en: AI modified one file but forgot the other 19 related ones. AI code has 4x the duplication rate of human code. 65% of developers say AI refactoring misses context. Build a dedicated consistency-guard agent — after every AI modification, auto-scan for global invariants, type consistency, and API contracts, and block problems before the PR is submitted.
evidence: SPK-120 跨文件一致性是AI最容易出错的地方
SPK-136 AI代码4倍重复率
SPK-146 65%开发者报告重构遗漏上下文
SPK-142 '生产噪音'——AI堆砌无用代码
evidence_en: SPK-120 — cross-file consistency is where AI most commonly fails
SPK-136 — AI code has 4x the duplication rate
SPK-146 — 65% of developers report refactoring context misses
SPK-142 — 'production noise': AI piling up useless code
first_step:
**做什么**：构建一个Git pre-commit hook脚本，对每次提交涉及的文件用代码分析器提取修改了哪些公共接口（函数签名、类型定义、导出常量），然后扫描整个仓库找到所有调用方，检查是否有未同步更新的引用，如有则阻止提交并给出具体提示。

**为什么做**：pre-commit hook是零侵入式的切入点——开发者安装后不需要改变任何工作习惯，每次提交自动检查。这也是最容易在团队中推广的形式，一个人装了觉得有用就会推荐给全团队。

**谁来做/怎么做**：一名熟悉AST解析的工程师，2-3周。先支持TypeScript/JavaScript（用ts-morph解析），后续扩展Python和Go。用Git的diff信息缩小扫描范围提升速度，输出友好的修复建议（“你修改了utils.ts第45行的函数签名，但api/routes.ts第12行还在用旧签名”）。
first_step_en:
**What**: Build a Git pre-commit hook that, for files touched by each commit, uses a code analyzer to extract which public interfaces changed (function signatures, type definitions, exported constants), then scans the entire repo to find all callers and checks for out-of-sync references. If any exist, block the commit with specific guidance.

**Why**: Pre-commit hook is a zero-intrusion wedge — once installed, developers change nothing in their workflow and every commit gets checked automatically. It's also the easiest form to spread within a team — one person installs it, finds it useful, and recommends it to the whole team.

**Who/How**: One engineer familiar with AST parsing, 2-3 weeks. Support TypeScript/JavaScript first (via ts-morph), extend to Python and Go later. Use Git's diff info to narrow scan scope and improve speed. Output friendly fix suggestions ('you modified the function signature at utils.ts:45, but api/routes.ts:12 still uses the old signature').
cc_anchor: 05_工具运行时.md — StreamingToolExecutor的isConcurrencySafe分类思路 + 文件历史系统的对话轮次级快照
cc_anchor_en: 05_Tool_Runtime.md — StreamingToolExecutor's isConcurrencySafe classification approach + file-history system's per-turn snapshots
plain_explanation: 想象你在一栋大楼里换了大门的锁，但忘了给保安、物业、停车场管理员都更新新钥匙。结果第二天保安进不了门、快递员无法送货、停车场闸机打不开——一个地方改了，连锁反应导致到处出问题。AI写代码经常犯的就是这个错误：修改了一个文件里的接口定义，但忘了同步更新其他19个引用这个接口的文件。而且AI生成的代码重复率是人类的4倍——同一段逻辑被复制到多个地方，改了一处忘了其他。BLP-019要做的就是一个“换锁提醒员”：每次有人（AI或人）修改代码时，自动检查所有关联的地方是否也更新了。
plain_explanation_en: Imagine changing the main-entrance lock in a building but forgetting to give new keys to security, the property manager, and the parking attendant. Next morning security can't get in, deliveries can't enter, the parking gate won't open — you changed one thing, and the chain reaction broke a dozen others. AI writing code makes this exact mistake: it modifies an interface definition in one file but forgets to sync the other 19 files that use it. And AI-generated code has 4x the duplication rate of human code — the same logic copied into multiple places, with modifications to one forgetting the others. BLP-019 is the 'lock-change reminder': every time someone (AI or human) modifies code, it auto-checks whether all connected places were also updated.
why_it_matters:
**市场需求**：AI辅助编程的普及速度远超AI代码质量工具的发展。65%的开发者报告AI重构会遗漏上下文，但目前的解决方案只是“让AI重新跑一遍”或“人工逐文件检查”——前者不可靠，后者耗时。

**竞争空白**：ESLint/TypeScript编译器能检查单文件内的错误，但跨文件一致性（“你改了接口但调用方没更新”）不在它们的能力范围内。现有CI/CD流程中没有专门针对这个问题的工具。

**时机窗口**：AI代码的比例在快速增长（Anthropic报告AI代码已占部分项目新增代码的30%+），跨文件不一致问题只会越来越严重。现在做这个工具，正好踩在问题变成产业级痛点的前夜。
why_it_matters_en:
**Market Need**: AI-assisted coding is spreading far faster than AI code-quality tooling. 65% of developers report AI refactoring misses context, but the only current solutions are 'run AI again' or 'check every file by hand' — the first is unreliable, the second is expensive.

**Competitive Gap**: ESLint and the TypeScript compiler check single-file errors, but cross-file consistency ('you changed the interface, callers didn't update') is outside their scope. Today's CI/CD pipelines have no dedicated tool for this problem.

**Timing Window**: AI-generated code share is growing fast (Anthropic reports AI code is already 30%+ of new code in some projects). Cross-file inconsistency will only get worse. Building this tool now lands right before the problem becomes industry-wide acute pain.

user_feedback.comment: pre-commit hook是极好的分发方式——零服务器成本、零注册门槛，用户安装就能用。变现路径可以是：开源基础版+企业版（支持自定义规则、团队统一配置、违规报告仪表盘）。核心挑战是性能——大型monorepo扫描全仓库可能需要数十秒，这会严重影响开发者体验。
user_feedback.comment_en: Pre-commit hook is a great distribution mechanism — zero server cost, zero signup friction, install and go. Monetization path: open-source free tier plus an enterprise version (custom rules, team-unified config, violation-report dashboard). The core challenge is performance — scanning a large monorepo may take tens of seconds, and that seriously degrades developer experience.

user_feedback.comment: 代码质量工具是被验证的商业模式（SonarQube、Snyk都是10亿美元级公司），AI代码质量是这个赛道的自然延伸。关键问题是能否做成平台而非单点工具——如果只做“跨文件一致性”，市场太窄；如果能扩展成“AI代码质量全套检测”，就有平台潜力。
user_feedback.comment_en: Code-quality tools are a validated business model (SonarQube, Snyk are both billion-dollar companies), and AI code quality is the natural extension. The key question is whether this becomes a platform or a point tool — 'cross-file consistency' alone is too narrow a market; extend to 'full-stack AI code-quality detection' and there's real platform potential.

user_feedback.comment: 上周刚被这个问题坑了：Cursor帮我重构了一个工具函数的参数顺序，但只改了定义处和2个调用方，遗漏了另外8个调用方。CI全绿是因为类型没变只是参数位置变了。如果有工具能在提交前抓住这种问题，我立刻安装。但hook不能太慢——超过5秒我就会想办法绕过去。
user_feedback.comment_en: I just got burned by this last week: Cursor refactored the parameter order of a utility function but only updated the definition and 2 callers — missed the other 8. CI was all green because types didn't change, only parameter positions did. If a tool caught this pre-commit, I'd install it immediately. But the hook can't be slow — if it takes more than 5 seconds I'll find a way around it.

user_feedback.comment: AST解析的准确性直接决定工具的可信度。TypeScript有ts-morph这种成熟库，但Python和Go的AST工具生态差异很大，跨语言支持是大量工作。另外动态语言（Python/JavaScript）中很多引用关系在静态分析阶段无法确定——函数作为参数传递、动态导入等场景都会是误报来源。需要在误报率和漏报率之间做平衡。
user_feedback.comment_en: AST parser accuracy directly determines tool credibility. TypeScript has mature libraries like ts-morph, but Python and Go have very different AST-tool ecosystems — cross-language support is a lot of work. Also, in dynamic languages (Python/JavaScript), many reference relationships can't be determined at static-analysis time — functions-as-parameters, dynamic imports, etc. will all be false-positive sources. You have to balance false-positive rate against false-negative rate carefully.

title: 离线AI Agent安全策略引擎
title_en: Offline AI Agent Safety Policy Engine
description: AI Agent正在从云端走向边缘（手机、汽车、工厂），这些环境经常离线。CC的killswitch在离线时fail-open——远程安全控制完全失效。做一个三层安全策略引擎：L0硬编码本地规则+L1带TTL的缓存策略+L2实时远程策略，离线时自动降级而不是全部失效。
description_en: AI agents are migrating from cloud to edge (phones, cars, factories), and edge environments are frequently offline. CC's killswitch fails open when offline — remote safety controls fully disappear. Build a three-tier policy engine: L0 hard-coded local rules, L1 cached policy with TTL, L2 live remote policy — degrading gracefully when offline instead of losing all controls.
evidence: CC的bypassPermissionsKillswitch离线时返回false=fail-open
SPK-215沙箱的failIfUnavailable默认false是同类问题
边缘AI部署(手机/汽车/工厂)正在快速增长
军事/医疗场景对离线安全有刚需
evidence_en: CC's bypassPermissionsKillswitch returns false offline = fails open
SPK-215 — the sandbox's failIfUnavailable defaults to false, same class of problem
Edge AI deployment (phones, cars, factories) is growing rapidly
Military and medical scenarios have hard requirements for offline safety
first_step:
**做什么**：设计并实现一个策略引擎SDK，支持三层策略存储——本地硬编码的绝对底线规则、带有效期的缓存策略、实时远程策略——当网络不可用时自动降级到缓存层甚至本地层，而不是完全放开所有权限。配三个场景Demo展示降级行为。

**为什么做**：边缘AI部署的安全模型和云端AI完全不同——云端可以依赖实时远程控制，边缘必须在断网时也能保证最低安全标准。这个需求在IoT、车载AI、工业控制场景中是刚需，且目前没有标准方案。

**谁来做/怎么做**：一名有Rust经验的系统工程师，6-8周。核心策略引擎用Rust实现（性能+跨平台编译需求），初版先提供一种语言绑定（推荐Node.js），配一个Demo场景（IoT设备断网权限降级）验证概念。在嵌入式开发社群和AI安全论坛发布，根据反馈再决定是否扩展Python绑定和更多场景。
first_step_en:
**What**: Design and implement a policy engine SDK supporting three storage tiers — locally hard-coded absolute baseline rules, cached policy with TTL, live remote policy — that automatically degrades to the cached tier or even the local tier when the network is unavailable, rather than opening up all permissions. Ship three scenario demos showing degradation behavior.

**Why**: The security model for edge AI deployment is fundamentally different from cloud AI. Cloud can depend on real-time remote controls; edge must maintain a minimum safety standard even when offline. This is a hard requirement in IoT, in-vehicle AI, and industrial-control scenarios, and no standard solution exists today.

**Who/How**: One systems engineer with Rust experience, 6-8 weeks. Implement the core policy engine in Rust (for performance and cross-platform compilation). Ship the first version with one language binding (recommend Node.js) and a single demo scenario (IoT device permission degradation when offline) to prove the concept. Release in embedded-dev communities and AI security forums, expand Python bindings and more scenarios based on feedback.

plain_explanation: 想象你家的智能门锁平时通过手机App远程控制——你可以随时改密码、给客人临时授权、查看开门记录。但有一天家里断网了。现在的大多数智能锁在断网时有两种做法：要么完全锁死（谁都进不了），要么完全放开（谁都进得了）。两种都不好。理想的方案是：断网时自动切到一套预存的基本规则——主人指纹照常开门、临时客人密码如果没过期也能用、但远程新授权就暂时不行了。AI安全系统现在就面临同样的问题：联网时有各种远程安全控制，断网时这些控制全部失效。BLP-020要做一个三层的安全策略引擎，断网时不是“全关”或“全开”而是智能降级。
plain_explanation_en: Imagine your home's smart lock is normally controlled remotely via a phone app — you can change passwords anytime, grant temporary guest access, review entry logs. Then one day your home internet goes down. Most smart locks do one of two things when offline: fully lock down (nobody in) or fully open up (anyone in). Both are bad. The ideal: when offline, automatically fall back to a pre-stored set of basic rules — owner fingerprint still works, unexpired guest codes still work, but new remote grants are temporarily suspended. AI safety systems face the identical problem today: when connected, various remote safety controls are in place; when disconnected, all of them fail. BLP-020 is a three-tier safety policy engine that, when offline, doesn't do 'fully closed' or 'fully open' but intelligent degradation.
why_it_matters:
**市场需求**：AI正在从云端扩展到边缘设备——车载AI、手机助手、工厂自动化、医疗设备。这些场景的共同特点是网络不稳定甚至完全离线。所有依赖“远程实时控制”的安全方案在这些场景中都会失效，这是一个结构性缺陷，不是边缘情况。

**竞争空白**：云端AI安全工具众多（Invariant Labs、Protect AI等），但专门解决“离线/弱网环境下的AI安全策略”的产品为零。军事和医疗场景对离线安全有强制性合规要求但找不到现成方案。

**时机窗口**：2025-2026年是边缘AI部署的爆发期（Apple Intelligence、高通AI引擎等都在推动端侧AI），离线安全会从“理论问题”变成“工程必须解决的问题”。现在做的人能成为这个细分方向的事实标准制定者。
why_it_matters_en:
**Market Need**: AI is expanding from cloud to edge devices — in-vehicle AI, phone assistants, factory automation, medical devices. The common trait of these scenarios is unstable or completely absent network connectivity. Every safety scheme that depends on 'live remote control' fails in these scenarios. This is a structural defect, not an edge case.

**Competitive Gap**: Cloud AI safety tools are abundant (Invariant Labs, Protect AI, etc.), but products specifically solving 'AI safety policy in offline/weak-network environments' are nonexistent. Military and medical scenarios have mandatory compliance requirements for offline safety and can't find off-the-shelf solutions.

**Timing Window**: 2025-2026 is the breakout period for edge AI deployment (Apple Intelligence, Qualcomm AI Engine, etc. are pushing on-device AI). Offline safety will shift from 'theoretical problem' to 'engineering must-solve.' Whoever builds this now can become the de facto standard-setter for this sub-direction.

user_feedback.comment: 边缘AI安全是一个高度垂直的市场，客户画像清晰（IoT厂商、车企、医疗设备公司），但销售周期长、集成复杂度高。建议不要一开始就做通用SDK，先深度绑定一个垂直场景（推荐IoT——设备量大、集成相对简单）做出参考实现，再横向复制到其他场景。
user_feedback.comment_en: Edge AI safety is a highly vertical market with clear customer profiles (IoT vendors, automakers, medical device companies), but sales cycles are long and integration complexity is high. Don't start with a generic SDK — deeply partner with one vertical (IoT is the pick — high device volume, relatively simple integration), produce a reference implementation, then replicate horizontally.

user_feedback.comment: 方向正确但市场时机可能偏早——2025年边缘AI的实际部署量还没有大到催生安全工具的规模化需求。如果是Seed/Pre-A阶段可以投赌方向，但需要团队能撑到市场成熟。Rust实现是加分项——嵌入式领域对性能和跨平台编译有刚需，这是技术壁垒。
user_feedback.comment_en: Direction is right but timing may be early — in 2025, edge AI's actual deployment volume isn't large enough yet to drive scaled safety-tool demand. At Seed or Pre-A, it's fine to bet on the direction, but the team needs to survive long enough for the market to mature. Rust implementation is a plus — embedded has hard requirements on performance and cross-platform compilation, and that's a technical moat.

user_feedback.comment: 我们正在做一个工厂巡检AI机器人，厂区WiFi覆盖不完整，机器人经常断网。目前的解决方案是“断网时回到固定巡检路线”，完全不智能。如果有一个安全策略引擎能让机器人在断网时自动降级到“只执行低风险操作”而不是完全停止工作，我们非常愿意集成。
user_feedback.comment_en: We're building a factory-inspection AI robot. WiFi coverage is incomplete and the robot frequently loses connectivity. Our current fix is 'fall back to a fixed patrol route when offline,' which isn't intelligent at all. If a safety policy engine let the robot auto-degrade to 'only execute low-risk operations' instead of stopping entirely, we'd happily integrate it.

user_feedback.comment: 三层策略的降级逻辑看似简单，实际工程细节很多：TTL过期后是fail-open还是fail-close？缓存层的策略如何防篡改？L0硬编码层的规则更新需要固件升级吗？每一个设计决策都会影响安全模型的可信度。建议在技术白皮书中先定义清晰的安全模型，再开始写代码。
user_feedback.comment_en: The three-tier degradation logic looks simple but has many engineering subtleties: after TTL expiry, fail-open or fail-close? How do you anti-tamper the cached-policy layer? Does an L0 hard-coded rule update require a firmware upgrade? Each design decision affects the credibility of the safety model. Define a clear threat model in a technical white paper before writing code.

title: AI苏格拉底式编程教练——学会而不是依赖
title_en: Socratic AI Programming Coach: Learn, Don't Depend
description: Anthropic自己的研究说AI辅助降低学习效果17%。CC的Learning模式用TODO(human)让AI搭脚手架而不是给答案。把这个模式做成一个完整的编程教育产品：根据学生水平动态调节'帮多少'，让人真正学会而不是永远依赖AI。
description_en: Anthropic's own research shows AI assistance reduces learning outcomes by 17%. CC's Learning mode uses TODO(human) to have the AI scaffold rather than give answers. Turn this pattern into a complete programming education product: dynamically adjust how much help to give based on the student's level, so people actually learn instead of becoming permanently dependent on AI.
evidence: Anthropic研究：AI辅助降低学习测试得分17%
CC的Learning模式+TODO(human)协议已有技术实现
SPK-141描述了'学习模式'的产品设想
Vygotsky ZPD理论提供了教育学理论基础
evidence_en: Anthropic research: AI assistance reduces learning test scores by 17%
CC's Learning mode + TODO(human) protocol already has a technical implementation
SPK-141 describes the 'learning mode' product concept
Vygotsky's Zone of Proximal Development provides the pedagogical theoretical foundation
first_step:
**做什么**：做一个VS Code扩展的概念验证版：用户开启“学习模式”后，AI生成的代码自动在关键位置插入TODO注释标记，提示用户“这里需要你自己完成”，同时在侧边栏提供分层提示（先给方向→再给伪代码→最后给答案）。

**为什么做**：验证两个核心假设：（1）学生是否愿意使用一个“故意不给完整答案”的AI工具，（2）分层提示的设计是否真的比直接给代码更有助于学习。一个简单的扩展就能开始收集真实用户行为数据。

**谁来做/怎么做**：一名熟悉VS Code Extension API的开发者，2周完成MVP。核心是一个提示词模板（让AI生成带TODO标记的代码）+ 侧边栏的分层提示UI。先在编程教学社区（FreeCodeCamp中文社区、编程学习群）发布beta版，观察3周内的安装留存率。
first_step_en:
**What**: Build a VS Code extension proof-of-concept: when the user enables Learning Mode, AI-generated code auto-inserts TODO comments at key positions marking 'you need to complete this yourself,' while the sidebar provides tiered hints (direction first → pseudo-code → full answer).

**Why**: Validate two core hypotheses: (1) will students actually use an AI tool that deliberately withholds complete answers? (2) does tiered-hint design actually help learning more than just giving code? A simple extension is enough to start collecting real user behavior data.

**Who/How**: One developer familiar with the VS Code Extension API, 2 weeks to MVP. The core is a prompt template (make the AI generate code with TODO markers) plus a sidebar tiered-hint UI. Release the beta in coding-education communities (FreeCodeCamp community, programming-learning groups) and track 3-week installation retention.
cc_anchor: 16_输出样式系统完全解析.md — Learning模式 + TODO(human)协议 + Default/Explanatory/Learning三种样式切换
cc_anchor_en: 16_Output_Style_System_Deep_Dive.md — Learning mode + TODO(human) protocol + Default/Explanatory/Learning three-style switching
plain_explanation: 学骑自行车最好的方式不是永远有人在后面扶着你——那样你永远学不会平衡。好的教练会先扶着你，然后悄悄放手，让你自己骑几米，摔了再扶起来教你调整。现在的AI编程工具就像一个永远扶着你的人：你说“帮我写个排序算法”，它直接给你完整代码。结果呢？Anthropic自己的研究发现用AI辅助学习的学生考试成绩反而低了17%——因为他们只是在“复制粘贴”而不是“真正理解”。BLP-021要做的是一个“苏格拉底式教练”：不直接给答案，而是给提示、给方向、给脚手架，根据你的水平调节“帮多少”，让你从“用AI的拐杖”变成“真正掌握技能”。
plain_explanation_en: The best way to learn to ride a bike isn't having someone hold you up forever — you'd never learn balance. A good coach holds on at first, then quietly lets go, lets you ride a few meters yourself, helps you up when you fall, teaches you to adjust. Today's AI coding tools are like someone who never lets go. You say 'write me a sorting algorithm,' and it hands you the complete code. The result? Anthropic's own research found students using AI assistance scored 17% *lower* on learning tests — because they were copy-pasting, not actually understanding. BLP-021 builds a Socratic coach: instead of giving answers, it gives hints, directions, and scaffolds, adjusting how much help to provide based on your skill level — so you move from 'AI crutch user' to 'actually mastered the skill.'
why_it_matters:
**市场需求**：编程教育是千亿级市场，AI辅助编程工具的普及使得“如何避免AI让学生变笨”成为教育界的紧迫议题。大学教授、编程训练营、企业培训部门都在寻找“既用AI提高效率又不损害学习效果”的解决方案。

**竞争空白**：Copilot、Cursor等工具都是“效率优先”设计，没有任何产品从“学习效果优先”角度设计AI编程辅助。这意味着整个“AI教育辅助”细分可以被重新定义。

**时机窗口**：Anthropic的17%研究在教育界引发了强烈反响，“AI影响学习”的话题正在成为主流讨论。现在做出一个有数据支撑的“学习优先”AI工具，既有商业价值也有社会影响力。
why_it_matters_en:
**Market Need**: Coding education is a multi-hundred-billion-dollar market, and the spread of AI coding tools has made 'how do we keep AI from making students dumber' an urgent issue in education. University professors, coding bootcamps, and enterprise training departments are all searching for 'use AI for efficiency without hurting learning outcomes' solutions.

**Competitive Gap**: Copilot, Cursor, and others are all designed efficiency-first. No product designs AI coding assistance learning-outcomes-first. This means the entire 'AI education assistance' subcategory is open for redefinition.

**Timing Window**: Anthropic's 17% finding has reverberated strongly in education. 'AI's impact on learning' is becoming mainstream discussion. Building a data-backed learning-first AI tool right now has both commercial value and social impact.

user_feedback.comment: 教育产品的变现逻辑清晰但天花板有限。个人用户付费意愿低，但B端（大学、培训营、企业培训）可以谈年费。核心问题是：用户会不会嫌“学习模式”太慢而直接去用Copilot？如果答案是“会”，这个产品只能作为教育场景的专用工具而非通用编程助手。
user_feedback.comment_en: Education products have clear monetization logic but a limited ceiling. Individual willingness-to-pay is low, but B2B (universities, bootcamps, enterprise training) can do annual contracts. The core question: will users find 'Learning Mode' too slow and just switch to Copilot? If the answer is yes, this is an education-scenario specialty tool, not a general coding assistant.

user_feedback.comment: 教育科技在2022-2023年经历了泡沫破裂，投资人对这个赛道普遍谨慎。但“AI + 教育”是一个新的故事——如果能用数据证明“我们的工具让学生学得更快更好”，可以重新打开投资人的兴趣。关键是必须有对照实验数据，光有产品概念不够。
user_feedback.comment_en: Edtech went through a bubble burst in 2022-2023 and investors are broadly cautious on the space. But 'AI + education' is a new story — if you can prove with data that your tool helps students learn faster and better, you can reopen investor interest. The key: controlled-experiment data. Product concept alone isn't enough.

user_feedback.comment: 作为编程新手，说实话我更想要的是“快速把项目跑起来”而不是“慢慢学习每一步”。但如果这个工具能让我在用AI的同时真正理解代码而不只是复制粘贴，长远来看是有价值的。关键是“学习模式”的节奏不能太慢——如果一个简单功能需要我填20个TODO，我会放弃。
user_feedback.comment_en: As a coding newbie, honestly I want 'get my project running fast' more than 'slowly learn every step.' But if this tool helps me actually understand the code while using AI instead of just copy-pasting, that's valuable long-term. The key: Learning Mode's pacing can't be too slow — if a simple feature requires filling in 20 TODOs, I'll quit.

user_feedback.comment: TODO(human)协议的核心挑战是“在哪里插TODO”的智能度——插在太简单的地方用户觉得被侮辱，插在太难的地方用户卡住放弃。这需要一个水平评估模型，根据用户历史完成情况动态调整难度。初版可以用简单规则（所有函数体留空），但长期需要个性化适应。
user_feedback.comment_en: The core challenge with TODO(human) is the intelligence of 'where to insert TODOs.' Too-easy spots feel insulting to the user, too-hard spots get them stuck and they quit. You need a skill-assessment model that dynamically adjusts difficulty based on the user's completion history. V1 can use simple rules (leave all function bodies empty), but long-term, personalized adaptation is required.

title: 中国AI编程开发者工具链——填补地理封锁造成的服务空白
title_en: AI Coding Toolchain for Chinese Developers — Filling the Gap Left by Geo-Blocking
description: 中国开发者用Claude Code的第一道门槛是科学上网而不是技术能力。模型被暗降、路由不透明、社区资源全是英文。做一个专门服务中文开发者的AI编程工具链：本地化配置指南+模型透明度监控+中文社区+成本优化。
description_en: For Chinese developers, the first barrier to using Claude Code is VPN access, not technical skill. Models get silently downgraded, routing is opaque, and community resources are English-only. Build an AI coding toolchain specifically serving Chinese developers: localized configuration guides, model transparency monitoring, a Chinese-language community, and cost optimization.
evidence: SPK-105 中国用户基础设施障碍被英文社区完全忽视
SPK-106 Cursor暗降模型损害用户信任
SPK-145 CC用户被错误路由到不同后端模型
字节Trae正在填这个空白说明市场真实存在
evidence_en: SPK-105 — infrastructure barriers for Chinese users are completely ignored by the English-speaking community
SPK-106 — Cursor's silent model downgrading erodes user trust
SPK-145 — CC users get incorrectly routed to different backend models
ByteDance's Trae filling this gap proves the market is real
first_step:
**做什么**：建一个中文知识库站点，收集50个最常见的Claude Code和Cursor配置问题的中文解决方案（如代理设置、API Key获取、模型选择、成本控制），配一个模型调用审计脚本——记录每次API调用的实际模型名和响应时间，让用户自己验证“我用的真的是GPT-4o/Opus还是被暗降了”。

**为什么做**：中文开发者的第一需求不是“更好的AI工具”而是“现有AI工具在中国环境下能正常工作”。解决这个基础问题就能建立巨大的社区信任和用户粘性。模型审计脚本是独特的差异化——没有任何英文社区关注这个问题。

**谁来做/怎么做**：1名内容编辑+1名工程师，2周完成站点v0.1+审计脚本。站点用VitePress或Docusaurus部署到Vercel/Cloudflare Pages（确保中国可访问），FAQ内容从中文社区（V2EX、掘金、知乎）的高赞回答整理。审计脚本用Python编写，拦截AI SDK的HTTP请求记录实际模型名。
first_step_en:
**What**: Build a Chinese-language knowledge base collecting the 50 most common Claude Code and Cursor configuration issues and their Chinese-language solutions (proxy setup, API key acquisition, model selection, cost control). Ship a model-call audit script that logs the actual model name and response time for each API call, letting users verify 'am I actually getting GPT-4o / Opus, or was I silently downgraded?'

**Why**: The first need of Chinese developers isn't a 'better AI tool' — it's 'existing AI tools working normally in the Chinese network environment.' Solving this foundational problem builds massive community trust and stickiness. The model-audit script is unique differentiation — no English-speaking community is paying attention to this problem.

**Who/How**: 1 content editor + 1 engineer, 2 weeks to site v0.1 and audit script. Use VitePress or Docusaurus deployed to Vercel/Cloudflare Pages (so the site loads reliably from within China); curate FAQ content from top-voted answers on Chinese communities. Write the audit script in Python, intercepting AI SDK HTTP requests to log the actual model name.
cc_anchor: 横切关注点章节 — USER_TYPE/feature gate机制揭示内外版差异 + 遥测系统揭示模型路由不透明
cc_anchor_en: Cross-cutting Concerns chapter — USER_TYPE/feature-gate mechanism reveals internal-vs-public version differences + telemetry system reveals opaque model routing
plain_explanation: 想象你搬到了一个新城市，但发现这里的公共交通系统所有站牌都是外语、公交卡只能用外国银行卡充值、而且高峰期某些线路会偷偷用没有空调的旧车替换——但官方从来不告诉你。你最需要的不是“更好的交通系统”，而是一份你看得懂的乘车指南、一个能用本地银行卡充值的渠道、和一个能告诉你“今天这趟车是不是换了旧车”的信息工具。中国开发者用AI编程工具面临的就是这个处境：工具本身很好，但配置说明全是英文、付费渠道不支持中国银行卡、而且你不知道自己实际用到的是最新模型还是被悄悄降级了。BLP-022要做的就是：中文指南+本地支付方案+模型透明度监控。
plain_explanation_en: Imagine moving to a new city and finding that all the public transit signs are in a foreign language, the transit card only accepts foreign bank cards for top-up, and during peak hours some lines quietly swap in older buses without air conditioning — but the transit authority never tells you. What you actually need isn't 'a better transit system' — it's a transit guide you can read, a top-up channel that accepts local bank cards, and a tool that tells you 'is today's bus the old one again?' Chinese developers using AI coding tools face exactly this situation: the tools themselves are good, but the configuration docs are all in English, payment channels don't accept Chinese bank cards, and you don't know whether you're getting the latest model or a silent downgrade. BLP-022 delivers: Chinese-language guides + local payment solutions + model transparency monitoring.
why_it_matters:
**市场需求**：中国有数百万活跃的软件开发者，AI编程工具的渗透率正在快速上升，但几乎所有主流工具（Copilot、Cursor、Claude Code）的文档、社区、技术支持都以英文为主。这不是“小众需求”——这是数百万用户被系统性忽视的巨大缺口。

**竞争空白**：字节Trae在做中国市场的AI编程工具，但它是一个完整的IDE产品——没有人在做“帮中国开发者更好地使用现有AI工具”的辅助层。FAQ站+审计脚本+社区的组合在国内完全没有竞争者。

**时机窗口**：Claude Code在中国开发者社区的热度正在爆发式增长（本白皮书项目本身就是证据），但官方完全没有中文本地化的计划。这个服务空白在未来6-12个月只会越来越大。
why_it_matters_en:
**Market Need**: China has millions of active software developers, and AI coding tool penetration is rising fast, but nearly every mainstream tool (Copilot, Cursor, Claude Code) has English-first documentation, community, and support. This isn't a 'niche need' — it's millions of systematically ignored users.

**Competitive Gap**: ByteDance's Trae is building an AI coding tool for the China market, but it's a complete IDE product. No one is building a 'helping Chinese developers use existing AI tools better' layer. The FAQ-plus-audit-script-plus-community combination has zero domestic competition.

**Timing Window**: Claude Code's heat in Chinese developer communities is growing explosively (this white paper itself is evidence), but Anthropic has no Chinese localization plans. The service gap will only widen over the next 6-12 months.

user_feedback.comment: 这个方向的商业化路径不太明确——FAQ站和审计脚本很难直接收费。可能的模式是：用免费内容建立社区影响力→推出付费的“中国开发者AI工具配置一对一咨询”→后续做企业级的AI工具采购顾问。另一条路是做一个“中国AI工具导航站”，通过推荐链接佣金变现。需要想清楚做社区还是做产品。
user_feedback.comment_en: Monetization path isn't clear — FAQ sites and audit scripts are hard to charge for directly. Possible model: use free content to build community influence → launch paid '1:1 AI tool configuration consulting for Chinese developers' → evolve into enterprise AI tool procurement advisory. Alternative path: build a 'Chinese AI tool navigation site' monetized through referral-link commissions. Decide whether you're building a community or a product.

user_feedback.comment: 这更像是一个社区项目而非VC投资标的——天花板有限且商业模式不清晰。但如果能演变成“中国AI开发者的一站式入口”（类似少数派之于Mac用户），就有平台价值。我会关注的指标是：月活用户数和用户的付费转化意愿。做成了可以是很好的Bootstrap生意，但不是典型的风险投资方向。
user_feedback.comment_en: This looks more like a community project than a VC investment target — limited ceiling and unclear business model. But if it can evolve into 'the one-stop entry point for Chinese AI developers' (similar to Sspai for Mac users), there's platform value. The metrics I'd watch: MAU and paid-conversion willingness. Built well it could be a great bootstrap business, but not a typical venture-capital direction.

user_feedback.comment: FAQ站和审计脚本技术上都不难做，但维护成本容易被低估。AI工具更新频繁，配置方案可能每个月都要更新；审计脚本需要适配每个工具的API结构变化。如果维护跟不上，内容很快就过时了，反而损害信誉。建议从一开始就建立社区贡献机制而不是全靠核心团队维护。
user_feedback.comment_en: FAQ site and audit script are technically easy to build, but the maintenance cost gets systematically underestimated. AI tools update frequently, configuration guides may need monthly updates, and the audit script has to adapt to every tool's API structure changes. If maintenance falls behind, content goes stale fast and actively damages credibility. Build a community-contribution mechanism from day one — don't rely on the core team to maintain everything.
